mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, a sustained rise in the index of industrial production reflects resilient corporate output that offsets higher financing costs, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 60% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, which translates directly into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or quarterly GDP growth shows no significant correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side weakness and falling corporate output, triggering negative short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.
